metadata.title: The Six-Component Prompt
metadata.description_blurb: 'Improve this intro' is a wish, not a work order. The six-component table turns any vague ask into an inspectable specification.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

beats.narration_text: 'Improve this intro' is a wish. It is not a work order. The problem is not that Claude ignores vague prompts — the problem is that Claude fills in every gap with a best guess, and its best guess is not your best guess. The six-component table turns any vague ask into an inspectable specification before any draft is generated. This is Liam, in for Bear.

props.command: Evaluate the methods section below before rewriting anything. [paste]

beats.narration_text: The wish: 'Improve this.' The work order: task, context, source material, constraints, output format, and the criteria that define done. The wish gets Claude's best guess. The work order gets something you can inspect against the spec. The difference is not what Claude does — it is what you give it to work with.
beats.new_visual_element: Wish vs spec — before/after prompt pair

beats.narration_text: The six components: task, context, source material, constraints, output format, and evaluation criteria. Each is a separate slot. Omit task and Claude guesses the operation. Omit criteria and Claude guesses what 'good' means. The components are not a framework — they are a checklist that reveals which slots are empty.
beats.new_visual_element: Six-component table — all six slots labeled

beats.narration_text: The three most skipped: source material, constraints, and criteria. Omit source material and Claude draws on training data instead of your documents. Omit constraints and Claude invents the norms for the output. Omit criteria and there is no way to check the output against anything except your own gut.
beats.new_visual_element: Most-skipped components — source / constraints / criteria highlighted

beats.narration_text: One habit that changes everything: ask for diagnosis before the rewrite. 'Evaluate the methods section before rewriting anything.' You see the plan. You correct the plan. Then the rewrite happens against a plan you approved — not a plan Claude invented.
beats.new_visual_element: Diagnosis before rewrite — ask for the plan, not the artifact

beats.narration_text: The six components are not a ritual — they are the minimum that makes a prompt inspectable. Task, context, source, constraints, format, criteria. Fill them and the output is auditable. Leave them empty and the output is interpretable. Those are different products.
beats.new_visual_element: Artifact view — six-component table filled out

beats.narration_text: Fill all six for your next prompt. Paste this — Claude will walk you through each component before generating.

props.command: Before generating anything, help me fill six prompt components: (1) task with a testable verb, (2) context — what makes this task situation-specific, (3) source material I am providing, (4) constraints — what the output must not do, (5) output format, (6) evaluation criteria — what would make this output wrong. Ask me for each one.

beats.narration_text: If this turns your next wish into a work order, subscribe. Liam, in for Bear.
